coupling_variable.definition: Fraction of surface sites occupied by adsorbed precursor molecules, as per Langmuir adsorption.
coupling_variable.equation_context: adsorption loss term in the diffusion-reaction equation

annotation.uncertainty_note: Symbol 'θ' is used in the reaction sink term, and the model assumes Langmuir adsorption, implying 'θ' represents surface coverage.

transport_variable.definition: Gas-phase partial pressure of the precursor reactant A inside the channel.

molecular_diffusion_coefficient.definition: Diffusion coefficient describing molecule-molecule interactions in the gas phase.
molecular_diffusion_coefficient.equation_context: Bosanquet relation (Eq. 12), molecular diffusion coefficient equation (Eq. 14)

knudsen_diffusion_coefficient.definition: Diffusion coefficient describing molecule-wall interactions, dominating at low pressures.
knudsen_diffusion_coefficient.equation_context: Bosanquet relation (Eq. 12), Knudsen diffusion coefficient equation (Eq. 13)

effective_diffusion_coefficient.definition: Combined diffusion coefficient accounting for both molecular and Knudsen diffusion, used in the transport equation.

spatial_coordinate.definition: Axial coordinate along the length of the lateral high-aspect-ratio channel.

time.definition: Temporal coordinate, representing exposure time or pulse time.

diffusion_coefficient.definition: The effective diffusion coefficient used in Fick's second law of diffusion.

knudsen_number.physical_meaning: ratio of mean free path to characteristic length
knudsen_number.definition: Kn = l / D, where l is the mean free path and D is the characteristic limiting feature size.

mean_free_path.physical_meaning: mean free path of gas molecules
mean_free_path.definition: Average distance a molecule travels between collisions.
mean_free_path.equation_context: Knudsen number definition (S0055), mean free path equations (Eq. 1, Eq. 4)

pressure.definition: Total gas pressure or partial pressure of reactant/inert gas, influencing mean free path and molecular diffusion.
pressure.equation_context: mean free path equation (Eq. 1, Eq. 4), molecular diffusion coefficient (Eq. 14, Eq. 16)

height.definition: The height of the lateral high-aspect-ratio channel.
height.equation_context: Thiele modulus (Eq. 9), dimensionless distance (Eq. 17), Knudsen diffusion coefficient (Eq. 13)

radius.definition: Characteristic dimension of the feature, used in Knudsen number calculation.

annotation.uncertainty_note: The paper uses 'D' for characteristic feature size, which is represented by 'h' (hydraulic diameter) in calculations.

hydraulic_diameter.definition: Characteristic length scale of the lateral high-aspect-ratio structure, used in diffusion calculations.
hydraulic_diameter.equation_context: governing diffusion-reaction equation (Eq. 7), Knudsen diffusion coefficient (Eq. 13), Knudsen number calculation (S0116)

boundary_conditions.inlet_condition: p_A(x=0, t) = p_A0 (initial partial pressure of reactant A)
boundary_conditions.wall_condition: adsorption flux (Langmuir adsorption) as a sink term in the bulk equation

source_reference.paper_title: Simulated conformality of atomic layer deposition in lateral channels: the impact of the Knudsen number on the saturation profile characteristics †
source_reference.notes: The model uses an analytical approximation for the reactant gas pressure along the channel, simplifying Fick's second law into piecewise linear and exponential functions (Eq. 17, 18, 20).
